task_updates.executor_notes: Attempted the requested deploy with `npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc`, but `npx` could not reach `registry.npmjs.org` in this environment. Confirmed a system `supabase` binary exists at `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase` and retried with that binary directly; it failed immediately with `Access token not provided`, so the edge function was not deployed and I could not verify remote inclusion of the `templates.ts` and `index.ts` changes.
commands_run: npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `GenerateEffectResponse` so `code` is optional and the response can carry `message` plus `isQuestionResponse`. Added `agentMessage` state, clear-on-reset/new-generation behavior, and a dismissible inline message box. In `handleGenerate()`, question-only responses now set the message, clear the prompt, and return before mutating generated code, name, schema, or compile path; normal responses set the message and then continue the existing code-update flow with an explicit `response.code` guard. Verified by inspecting the `handleGenerate()` block, confirming dismiss/reset hooks in source, and running a local TypeScript `transpileModule` parse check with zero diagnostics.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: No. The edge-function deploy did not succeed in this environment: the requested `npx` invocation failed on npm registry DNS resolution, and a retry with the installed `supabase` CLI failed due a missing `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN`/login. Because deployment did not complete, I could not verify the remote project includes the latest `templates.ts` and `index.ts` changes.

Confirmed in `handleGenerate()` that the `isQuestionResponse` branch sets `agentMessage`, clears `prompt`, and returns before any `setCode`, `setParameterSchema`, name update, or compile call. Also confirmed `agentMessage` is cleared on new generation, inside `resetForm()`, and via the inline dismiss button.
